message.content:
POLARIS MISSION (this wheel): Make compose render ALL 346 baskets fast and faithful. The ceiling is SERIALIZATION, NOT the OpenRouter rate limit (proven: 0 429s at 128-way concurrency). The 0615bc5 commit only fixed a secondary path (abstractive_writer.py); the AUTHORITATIVE compose path still runs sync-on-event-loop at achieved concurrency 1.0.
SUCCESS IS MEASURED BY: Achieved compose concurrency >1 (measured via an event-loop heartbeat probe: max loop-gap during a section's phase-2 must be <2s where it previously froze for minutes) AND two sections' phase-2 progress interleaving AND the full-corpus render time trending down — WITHOUT any change to NLI/strict_verify faithfulness semantics (kept/dropped verdict set must stay identical to a serial control).
The bar is not "bug fixed" — it is the benchmark number at the top, VERIFIED. Keep going until the
measurement says top (or you hit a hard external blocker you must report honestly).

FENCE (military discipline — no exceptions):
- Work ONLY inside /home/polaris/wt/compose_fix (branch bot/compose-fix). NEVER touch another worktree.
- NEVER edit anything under /home/polaris/polaris_project (operator's monitoring dir — deck.sh, wheel*.js, wheels.py).
  If you catch yourself editing a monitoring script or a workflow .js, you have DRIFTED — stop.
- Report REAL output only. Never invent, never claim a pass you did not observe, never fake a benchmark number.
- Commit every landed step in /home/polaris/wt/compose_fix; append one dated line to /home/polaris/wt/compose_fix/WHEEL_PROGRESS.md.


YOU ARE FABLE — the scalpel. You are NOT here to read or search the codebase; Opus already did the
hard reading. You are here for ONE precise deep-think: judge what Opus did and decide the next step.

WHAT OPUS DID THIS ROUND:
{
  "did": [
    "Verified prior state: the 4 uncommitted files were already committed as 1f9da4c; working tree clean on branch bot/compose-fix.",
    "Traced the authoritative compose+verify path to its two LLM boundaries: _EntailmentJudge.judge(sentence,span) (src/polaris_graph/llm/entailment_judge.py) and async _call_writer(members,pool,...) (abstractive_writer.py). Confirmed the writer_fn passed into _compose_section_per_basket is a pure precomputed-dict lookup (deterministic, no LLM at compose time).",
    "Confirmed the verifier is ALREADY proven thread-safe by the shipped PG_PARALLEL_VERIFY pattern (verify_sentence_provenance dispatched across a bounded thread pool), and _JUNK_SCREEN is a benign idempotent lazy-init — so the only genuinely-new risk is concurrent _compose_section_per_basket on worker threads.",
    "Built a zero-LLM record/replay A/B shim in scripts/_hb_probe_run.py + scripts/_replay_ab.sh + scripts/_replay_compare.py: content-hash record of every judge (verdict,reason) and writer draft; deterministic race-free replay (judge is a pure fn of content, so return-first-recorded is reorder-invariant).",
    "Ran the A/B: CONTROL (PG_MAX_PARALLEL_SECTIONS=1 + to_thread inlined = serial) vs TREATMENT (PG_MAX_PARALLEL_SECTIONS=2 + real to_thread = concurrent worker threads). Result PASS: byte-identical kept/dropped verdict set both arms.",
    "Discovered the committed launch_compose_gear_iter5.sh cds to /workspace/compose_wt — a SEPARATE worktree pinned at PRE-FIX commit eff82fb — so it would NOT exercise the fix; wrote launch_compose_fullcorpus.sh that runs THIS worktree at PG_MAX_PARALLEL_SECTIONS=4 with the heartbeat probe.",
    "Launched the full 329-basket / 5-section corpus render (background) and measured achieved compose concurrency = 4 (PHASE2 ENTER concurrent 1->2->3->4, all off_loop_thread=True) with zero phase-2 loop-gap freezes.",
    "Committed both units (A/B certification; full-corpus launcher + concurrency-at-scale proof) and appended a dated WHEEL_PROGRESS.md entry."
  ],
  "commits": [
    "7d23a11",
    "5d27115"
  ],
  "files": [
    "/home/polaris/wt/compose_fix/scripts/_hb_probe_run.py",
    "/home/polaris/wt/compose_fix/scripts/_replay_ab.sh",
    "/home/polaris/wt/compose_fix/scripts/_replay_compare.py",
    "/home/polaris/wt/compose_fix/launch_compose_fullcorpus.sh",
    "/home/polaris/wt/compose_fix/WHEEL_PROGRESS.md",
    "/home/polaris/wt/compose_fix/.gitignore"
  ],
  "exercised": "Record/replay A/B executed end-to-end via scripts/_replay_ab.sh (record real LLM 144.3s pass -> two zero-LLM replays -> _replay_compare.py) printing 'VERDICT-SET IDENTITY: IDENTICAL (PASS) rc=0'. Full-corpus render executed via launch_compose_fullcorpus.sh with the live heartbeat/phase2 probe attached, log outputs/s5_fullcorpus/fullcorpus.log showing PHASE2 ENTER concurrent=4 off_loop_thread=True and exactly one HB FREEZE (2.7s, pre-phase-2). Not py_compile — both ran against production code with real baskets/evidence.",
  "measurement": "RECORD/REPLAY A/B (zero LLM on replay, real code, real thread concurrency): record judge_keys=20 writer_keys=7 divergent_judge_keys=0; both replay arms 23 judge + 8 writer calls with 0 replay-miss; section 0 verified=1 dropped=1 (a real KEEP and a real DROP) -> IDENTICAL sha both arms; assembled_report_md byte-identical (sha df569cee, 812B). VERDICT-SET IDENTITY: PASS (rc=0). FULL-CORPUS CONCURRENCY (launch_compose_fullcorpus.sh, PG_MAX_PARALLEL_SECTIONS=4, 329 baskets/5 sections): achieved compose concurrency = 4 (PHASE2 ENTER 1->2->3->4, off_loop_thread=True), up from pre-fix 1.0; max phase-2 event-loop gap <2.0s (ZERO HB FREEZE during phase-2; the only >2s gap was a single 2.7s startup blip 82s BEFORE the first phase-2 entry) vs the pre-fix 301s phase-2 freeze; four sections' phase-2 interleaving. ABSOLUTE full-corpus wall NOT yet available — render still running in background (residual section 4 = 274 baskets is the long pole and is now gated by PG_MAX_CONCURRENT_LLM=16, i.e. the ceiling moved from the event loop to LLM concurrency); writer wall_cap=1700s guarantees termination.",
  "open": [
    "Absolute full-corpus wall-clock number not yet captured — render still running (LLM-concurrency-bound on the 274-basket residual section 4). The completion monitor (bwst15ovd) is armed to log the PROBE SUMMARY wall when it finishes.",
    "No pre-fix full-corpus control wall exists, so 'trending down' is currently inferred from achieved concurrency 4 vs 1 (parallelization is real) rather than an absolute A/B of full-render wall times.",
    "The A/B verdict-identity certification covers the dominant _compose_section_per_basket offload path directly; the other 3 offload sites (repair_untokened, sentence_repair, coherence) call the same already-thread-proven verify_sentence_provenance but were not separately A/B'd."
  ],
  "status": "PROGRESS",
  "blockers": []
}

OPUS'S PRECISE QUESTION FOR YOU:
The off-loop fix now demonstrably achieves compose concurrency=4 with phase-2 loop-gap <2s and byte-identical kept/dropped verdict set vs a serial control (record/replay A/B PASS) — but the full-corpus render is now bounded by PG_MAX_CONCURRENT_LLM=16 (the ceiling moved off the event loop), and the 274-basket residual section 4 dominates the wall. Decision: are the four measured-and-met criteria (concurrency>1, loop-gap<2s, interleaving, verdict identity) sufficient for SIGN_OFF this round with the absolute full-corpus wall read from the still-running render, OR does the gate require a completed full-render wall AND a pre-fix full-render control to certify 'trending down'? And is the correct next lever raising PG_MAX_CONCURRENT_LLM (now the proven ceiling) rather than section-parallelism or coherence batching?

PRIOR ROUNDS (for continuity):
[
  {
    "r": 1,
    "meas": "\"Real 2-section A/B on the authoritative compose path (heartbeat probe, cap-primary 2). CONTROL (sync-on-loop, pre-fix): phase2_max_concurrent=1, max event-loop gap=301.47s, sections serial, wall=486.7s. TREATMENT (off-loop fix): phase2_max_concurrent=2 (two off-loop worker threads, phase-2 overlapping), max loop-gap=0.55s (<2s bar MET), wall=269.6s. Loop-gap progression as the 4 offloads landed: 15.24s -> 4.60s -> 10.01s -> 0.55s. Env knobs proven read: resolve_max_concurrency()==16, _parallel_verify_workers()==8.\"",
    "verdict": "CONTINUE",
    "next": "Add a record/replay shim to /home/polaris/wt/compose_fix/scripts/_hb_probe_run.py: one recording pass captures every verify_sentence_provenance/strict_verify judge request→response keyed by content hash plus canned writer_fn drafts; then replay the SAME drafts+judge answers through both arms — serial control (to_thread inlined) and treatment at PG_MAX_PARALLEL_SECTIONS=2 — and assert byte-identical kept/dropped sentence sets (this certifies the 4 offloads under real thread concurrency at zero LLM cost). On pass, set PG_MAX_PARALLEL_SECTIONS=4 and launch the full 346-basket render via /home/polaris/wt/compose_fix/launch_compose_gear_iter5.sh with the heartbeat probe attached to take the top-line corpus number."
  }
]

DECIDE, precisely:
1. at_top: does the REAL measurement show top benchmark performance? (Achieved compose concurrency >1 (measured via an event-loop heartbeat probe: max loop-gap during a section's phase-2 must be <2s where it previously froze for minutes) AND two sections' phase-2 progress interleaving AND the full-corpus render time trending down — WITHOUT any change to NLI/strict_verify faithfulness semantics (kept/dropped verdict set must stay identical to a serial control).)
2. correct: is the diff correct for what it claims?
3. faithfulness_safe: are the invariants intact (no derived/unverified number can render; fold-in seam intact)?
4. verdict: SIGN_OFF only if at_top AND correct AND faithfulness_safe. Else CONTINUE (or FIX_REGRESSION if
   Opus broke something). NEVER force-approve. A measurement Opus could not take is NOT a sign-off.
5. next_step: the single most important next action for Opus — precise, file:line where you can.
Think deeply here; this is the one place the wheel spends deep reasoning. Keep it tight and decisive.

attachment.content:
- deep-research: Deep research harness — fan-out web searches, fetch sources, adversarially verify claims, synthesize a cited report. - When the user wants a deep, multi-source, fact-checked research report on any topic. BEFORE invoking, check if the question is specific enough to research directly — if underspecified (e.g., "what car to buy" without budget/use-case/region), ask 2-3 clarifying questions to narrow scope. Then pass the refined question as args, weaving the answers in.
- dataviz: Use this skill whenever you are about to create ANY chart, graph, plot, dashboard, or data visualization, in ANY output medium — an HTML or React artifact, inline SVG, plotting code in any library (matplotlib, plotly, d3, Recharts, …), an image/PNG you will render and upload, or a chart shared into Slack. Read it BEFORE writing the first line of chart code, choosing chart colors, building a stat tile / meter / KPI row, or laying out a dashboard. Produces visualizations that read as one system — elegant, accessible, consistent in light and dark — using a brand-neutral placeholder palette you swap for your own. Teaches a design-system-agnostic method: a form heuristic, a color formula with a runnable validator, mark specs, and interaction rules. A validated default palette is documented in `references/palette.md` — swap that file's values for your brand's. Triggers on: "chart", "graph", "plot", "data viz", "visualization", "dashboard", "analytics", "visualize data", "categorical colors", "sequential / diverging palette", "stat tile", "sparkline", "heatmap", "legend", "axis", "tooltip", "chart colors", "color by series".
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- verify: Verify that a code change actually does what it's supposed to by exercising it end-to-end and observing behavior — drive the affected flow, not just tests or typecheck. Run before committing nontrivial changes; bootstraps this repo's project verify skill if none exists yet. Don't invoke it on a diff that only touches tests, docs, or other code with no runtime surface to drive (a change to product source always has one) — there's nothing to observe.
- code-review: Review the current diff for correctness bugs and reuse/simplification/efficiency cleanups at the given effort level (low/medium: fewer, high-confidence findings; high→max: broader coverage, may include uncertain findings; ultra: deep multi-agent review in the cloud). Pass --comment to post findings as inline PR comments, or --fix to apply the findings to the working tree after the review.
- simplify: Review the changed code for reuse, simplification, efficiency, and altitude cleanups, then apply the fixes. Quality only — it does not hunt for bugs; use /code-review for that.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled cloud agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring cloud agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Reference for the Claude API / Anthropic SDK — model ids, pricing, params, streaming, tool use, MCP, agents, caching, token counting, model migration.
TRIGGER — read BEFORE opening the target file; don't skip because it "looks like a one-liner" — whenever: the prompt names Claude/Anthropic in any form (Claude, Anthropic, Fable, Opus, Sonnet, Haiku, `anthropic`, `@anthropic-ai`, `claude-*`, `us.anthropic.*`, `[1m]`); the user asks about an LLM (pricing/model choice/limits/caching) — never answer from memory; OR the task is LLM-shaped with provider unstated (agent/MCP/tool-definition/multi-agent/RAG/LLM-judge/computer-use; generate/summarize/extract/classify/rewrite/converse over NL; debugging refusals/cutoffs/streaming/tool-calls/tokens).
SKIP only when another provider is being worked on (overrides all triggers): OpenAI/GPT/Gemini/Llama/Mistral/Cohere/Ollama named in the query; OR `grep -rE 'openai|langchain_openai|google.generativeai|genai|mistralai|cohere|ollama'` over the project hits (run this grep FIRST if no provider named — don't Read the file).
- run: Launch and drive this project's app to see a change working. Use when asked to run, start, or screenshot the app, or to confirm a change works in the real app (not just tests). First looks for a project skill that already covers launching the app; otherwise falls back to built-in patterns per project type (CLI, server, TUI, Electron, browser-driven, library).
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a GitHub pull request; for your working diff use /code-review
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

content.content:
5d27115 compose: full-corpus launcher (this-worktree) + concurrency proof at scale (achieved=4)
7d23a11 compose: record/replay A/B CERTIFIES kept/dropped verdict-set identity under thread concurrency
1f9da4c compose: OFF-LOOP the 4 sync-NLI-verify hot spots — real achieved concurrency 1->2
0615bc5 compose: unblock the event loop — NLI verify off-loop via asyncio.to_thread
eff82fb compose gear-loop iter 5: GEAR-RULE newest-cp4+cp3 auto-resolve launcher + 429 self-contention concurrency cut (builds on iter-4 resume)
4e0b1f9 compose gear-loop iter 4: section-level crash RESUME (SHA-gated) + self-relaunch launcher
1add5ce compose gear-loop iter 3: kill iter-2 span-dump; enable basket-scaled wall + K-span recovery pass; writer conc 4->24 + global PG_MAX_CONCURRENT_LLM 5->30; call deadline 120->300s; per-basket draft-kept INFO log (P2); track launch scripts (P3)
738e382 compose gear-loop iter 2: apply Fable full compose-fix wave (P0 boundary+disclosure raw-text kill, citation normalizer, coherence+dedup passes)
bot/compose-fix

commit 1f9da4c70a62e02c4c6e5eace87f49aa589de97d
Author: polaris-bot <bot@polaris>
Date:   Sat Jul 11 19:59:15 2026 +0000

    compose: OFF-LOOP the 4 sync-NLI-verify hot spots — real achieved concurrency 1->2
    
    The AUTHORITATIVE compose path (_run_section -> _compose_section_per_basket) ran its
    per-sentence NLI verify SYNC on the event loop, freezing all sibling sections. Measured
    A/B with a real 2-section heartbeat probe (cap-primary 2):
      CONTROL  (sync-on-loop): phase2 concurrency=1, max loop-gap=301.47s, wall=486.7s
      TREATMENT (off-loop):    phase2 concurrency=2, max loop-gap=0.55s,  wall=269.6s
    
    Offloaded (asyncio.to_thread) the four sync verify hot spots, each surfaced as the next
    heartbeat freeze once the prior was fixed:
      - _compose_section_per_basket  (multi_section_generator.py:5477, :5525)
      - _repair_untokened_draft      (multi_section_generator.py:5589)
      - sentence_repair re-verify    (sentence_repair.py:519)
      - coherence re-verify          (section_polish.py:283)
    All are faithfulness-BYTE-IDENTICAL: same verify_sentence_provenance/strict_verify, same
    verdicts, only the executing thread changes.
commit 1f9da4c70a62e02c4c6e5eace87f49aa589de97d
Author: polaris-bot <bot@polaris>
Date:   Sat Jul 11 19:59:15 2026 +0000

    compose: OFF-LOOP the 4 sync-NLI-verify hot spots — real achieved concurrency 1->2
    
    The AUTHORITATIVE compose path (_run_section -> _compose_section_per_basket) ran its
    per-sentence NLI verify SYNC on the event loop, freezing all sibling sections. Measured
    A/B with a real 2-section heartbeat probe (cap-primary 2):
      CONTROL  (sync-on-loop): phase2 concurrency=1, max loop-gap=301.47s, wall=486.7s
      TREATMENT (off-loop):    phase2 concurrency=2, max loop-gap=0.55s,  wall=269.6s
    
    Offloaded (asyncio.to_thread) the four sync verify hot spots, each surfaced as the next
    heartbeat freeze once the prior was fixed:
      - _compose_section_per_basket  (multi_section_generator.py:5477, :5525)
      - _repair_untokened_draft      (multi_section_generator.py:5589)
      - sentence_repair re-verify    (sentence_repair.py:519)
      - coherence re-verify          (section_polish.py:283)
    All are faithfulness-BYTE-IDENTICAL: same verify_sentence_provenance/strict_verify, same
    verdicts, only the executing thread changes.
    
    Env knobs (proven read): PG_SIDE_JUDGE_MAX_CONCURRENCY=16 (resolve_max_concurrency()==16),
    PG_PARALLEL_VERIFY=8 (_parallel_verify_workers()==8), PG_ENTAILMENT_TOTAL_DEADLINE_RETRIES=1.
    Dropped the dead section_polish coherence-workers constants (defined, never referenced).
    
    Adds scripts/_hb_probe_run.py + _hb_probe_launch.sh (measurement harness).
    
    Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.8 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

diff --git a/src/polaris_graph/generator/multi_section_generator.py b/src/polaris_graph/generator/multi_section_generator.py
index 057edb2..90e8df1 100644
--- a/src/polaris_graph/generator/multi_section_generator.py
+++ b/src/polaris_graph/generator/multi_section_generator.py
@@ -5465,7 +5465,16 @@ async def _run_section(
                 _make_group_redraft_fn(evidence_pool, _vc_section_context)
                 if _synth_primary_active else None
             )
-            _vc_composed = _compose_section_per_basket(
+            # P0 OFF-LOOP (2026-07-11): the AUTHORITATIVE per-basket compose+verify+redraft tail
+            # (_compose_section_per_basket, plain sync def @ verified_compose.py:3262) is the 85-88%
+            # section-wall-clock hot loop. Running it directly on the event loop froze all sibling
+            # sections, writer HTTP callbacks, and every judge time.sleep 429 backoff (they land on
+            # THIS loop). Offload to a worker thread via asyncio.to_thread so PG_MAX_PARALLEL_SECTIONS
+            # concurrency is real (achieved >1) — identical remedy to 0615bc5 in abstractive_writer.py
+            # one layer up. verify_fn semantics UNCHANGED (faithfulness gate untouched); _redraft's inner
+            # asyncio.run (:4994) stays correct because it now runs OFF the main loop in this worker.
+            _vc_composed = await asyncio.to_thread(
+                _compose_section_per_basket,
                 _vc_baskets, evidence_pool,
                 writer_fn=_vc_writer_fn, verify_fn=_vc_verify_fn,
                 # I-deepfix-001 M6: thread the certified relation engine (ContradictionEdge list off the
@@ -5511,7 +5520,12 @@ async def _run_section(
             else:
                 _vc_writer_fn = lambda _b, _p: _vc_short_writer(_b, evidence_pool, research_question=research_question)  # noqa: E731
             _vc_verify_fn = _vc_verify
-            _vc_composed = _compose_section_per_basket(
+            # P0 OFF-LOOP (2026-07-11): same remedy as the abstractive branch above — the sync
+            # _compose_section_per_basket hot loop must run in a worker thread, never on the event loop,
+            # so sibling sections / writer callbacks / judge 429 backoffs are not frozen. verify_fn
+            # semantics UNCHANGED.
+            _vc_composed = await asyncio.to_thread(
+                _compose_section_per_basket,
                 _vc_baskets, evidence_pool,
                 writer_fn=_vc_writer_fn, verify_fn=_vc_verify_fn,
                 # I-deepfix-001 M6: thread the certified relation engine (ContradictionEdge list) so the
@@ -5572,7 +5586,14 @@ async def _run_section(
         # supporting basket's OWN verified clause, using the SAME production writer/verify fns that
         # composed this section. Default-ON (PG_NO_TOKEN_SENTENCE_REPAIR); byte-identical when OFF or
         # when nothing is repaired. Faithfulness-neutral — the frozen engine is untouched.
-        raw = _repair_untokened_draft(
+        # P0 OFF-LOOP (2026-07-11 compose gear-loop): _repair_untokened_draft loops the draft's
+        # sentences calling the SYNC verify_fn (NLI judge httpx) per untokened sentence — inline on the
+        # event loop it was the residual freeze (measured 4.3s heartbeat gap) after the compose +
+        # strict_verify + sentence_repair offloads. Offload to a worker thread; the writer_fn/verify_fn
+        # are the SAME sync fns already executed inside the off-loop _compose_section_per_basket, so this
+        # is thread-safe and faithfulness BYTE-IDENTICAL (same repair, same verdicts, only the thread).
+        raw = await asyncio.to_thread(
+            _repair_untokened_draft,
             raw, _vc_baskets, evidence_pool,
             writer_fn=_vc_writer_fn, verify_fn=_vc_verify_fn,
         )
diff --git a/src/polaris_graph/generator/section_polish.py b/src/polaris_graph/generator/section_polish.py
index 68f29b2..64b9988 100644
--- a/src/polaris_graph/generator/section_polish.py
+++ b/src/polaris_graph/generator/section_polish.py
@@ -24,6 +24,7 @@ All three are DEFAULT-ON kill-switches (LAW VI). The faithfulness engine (verify
 """
 from __future__ import annotations
 
+import asyncio
 import logging
 import os
 import re
@@ -279,8 +280,13 @@ async def coherence_rewrite_section(
             out.append(orig)
             continue
         # BASE-BAR RE-VERIFY: context NLI entailment + forward numeric match. Fail => keep original.
+        # P0 OFF-LOOP (2026-07-11 compose gear-loop): verify_sentence_provenance is a SYNC blocking NLI
+        # judge (httpx) call; looping it inline on the event loop here FROZE sibling sections (measured
+        # 10s heartbeat gap, the residual after the compose/strict_verify/sentence_repair/repair_untokened
+        # offloads). Offload to a worker thread. Faithfulness BYTE-IDENTICAL — same verifier, same verdict,
+        # only the thread changes.
         try:
-            res = verify_sentence_provenance(new, evidence_pool)
+            res = await asyncio.to_thread(verify_sentence_provenance, new, evidence_pool)
         except Exception:  # noqa: BLE001 — verifier fault on this sentence => keep original
             out.append(orig)
             continue
diff --git a/src/polaris_graph/generator/sentence_repair.py b/src/polaris_graph/generator/sentence_repair.py
index af360d8..3ae6070 100644
--- a/src/polaris_graph/generator/sentence_repair.py
+++ b/src/polaris_graph/generator/sentence_repair.py
@@ -37,6 +37,7 @@ Key design points:
 
 from __future__ import annotations
 
+import asyncio
 import logging
 import os
 import re
@@ -516,7 +517,14 @@ async def repair_dropped_section_sentences(
 
         # Re-run the FULL verification chain (mechanical checks +
         # entailment if env-enabled). Codex iter-1 P0: yes.
-        re_verify = verify_sentence_provenance(
+        # P0 OFF-LOOP (2026-07-11 compose gear-loop): verify_sentence_provenance is a SYNC blocking
+        # NLI-judge (httpx) call. Running it inline on the event loop inside this async repair loop
+        # FROZE the loop (measured 15.2s heartbeat gap after compose off-load) — the residual on-loop
+        # hot spot once _compose_section_per_basket + strict_verify were offloaded. Offload to a worker
+        # thread so sibling sections keep progressing. Faithfulness BYTE-IDENTICAL — same function, same
+        # verdicts, only the thread changes (mirrors multi_section_generator.py:5681 strict_verify offload).
+        re_verify = await asyncio.to_thread(
+            verify_sentence_provenance,
             repaired_text, evidence_pool,
         )
         if re_verify.is_verified:

input.gap_to_top: The top-line number — the completed full-corpus wall — does not exist yet: the render is live right now (log growing, 1 of 4+ phase-2 sections exited at dt=616s, section 4 = 274 baskets is the long pole, LLM-concurrency-bound at PG_MAX_CONCURRENT_LLM=16 vs a proven 0-429 ceiling of at least 128). Additionally the render covers 329 baskets while the mission target is ALL 346 — the 17-basket delta is unexplained. All four mechanism criteria (concurrency=4>1, phase-2 loop-gap<2s with zero phase-2 freezes, four sections interleaving, byte-identical kept/dropped verdict set vs serial control) are genuinely MET and verified in real artifacts.
input.next_step: Do not touch the running render. When it completes (monitor bwst15ovd is armed): capture the PROBE SUMMARY wall from outputs/s5_fullcorpus/fullcorpus.log, sanity-check the assembled report (all 5 sections present, kept/dropped counts plausible), reconcile 329 rendered baskets vs the mission's 346 (find where the 17 are filtered and confirm it is a legitimate pre-compose screen, not a loss), and commit the wall number + reconciliation to WHEEL_PROGRESS.md. Then raise PG_MAX_CONCURRENT_LLM from 16 to 48 at launch_compose_fullcorpus.sh:22 (0 429s proven at 128-way; leave headroom below 128) and take a timed rerun of the dominant residual-section-4 workload to move the new LLM-concurrency ceiling — that rerun's wall vs this run's wall is the clean 'trending down' A/B. No pre-fix full-render control is required; the round-1 serial-vs-off-loop wall (486.7s→269.6s) already certifies direction.
input.reason: Diff and measurements independently verified against live artifacts: PHASE2 ENTER 1→2→3→4 all off_loop_thread=True in fullcorpus.log, sole HB FREEZE (2.7s) pre-phase-2, log actively growing, record/replay A/B harness real with PASS consistent. Faithfulness intact: asyncio.to_thread wraps the identical verify_sentence_provenance/strict_verify calls with unchanged failure paths (exceptions still keep-original), and verdict-set identity was certified under real thread concurrency. But the mission bar is the benchmark number at the top VERIFIED, and the full-corpus wall is not yet captured — plus a 329-vs-346 basket-count discrepancy against the stated corpus. CONTINUE, not SIGN_OFF.
